instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Entire Agreement in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: This Contract, including all schedules and amendments, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to its subject matter and supersedes all prior negotiations, representations, warranties, understandings, and agreements, whether written or oral.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Governing Law in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: This Contract shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Province of Ontario and the federal laws of Canada applicable therein. Each party irrevocably attorns to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Ontario.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Governing Law in CA-BC jurisdiction.
output: This Contract shall be governed by the laws of the Province of British Columbia and the federal laws of Canada applicable therein. The parties irrevocably submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of British Columbia sitting in Vancouver.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Governing Law in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: This Contract shall be interpreted and governed in accordance with the laws in force in the province or territory where the Work is performed, or if performed in more than one jurisdiction, the laws of Ontario, and the federal laws of Canada applicable therein.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Amendments in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: No amendment, modification, or supplement to this Contract shall be valid or binding unless set out in a formal written amendment executed by the duly authorized representatives of both parties. No waiver of any provision shall constitute a waiver of any other provision.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Severability in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: If any provision of this Contract is determined to be invalid or unenforceable by a court of competent jurisdiction, such determination shall not affect the validity or enforceability of the remaining provisions, which shall continue in full force and effect.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Waiver in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The failure of either party to insist upon strict performance of any term or condition of this Contract, or to exercise any right or remedy consequent upon breach, shall not constitute a waiver of any such term, condition, right, or remedy for that or any subsequent breach.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Assignment in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall not assign this Contract or any part thereof, or any benefit or interest therein, without the prior written consent of Canada. Any assignment made without such consent shall be void and of no effect. Canada may assign its rights under this Contract to any federal department or agency.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Notices in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: Any notice, demand, or other communication required or permitted under this Contract shall be in writing and shall be deemed to have been duly given when delivered personally, sent by registered mail, or transmitted by email with confirmed receipt to the addresses set out in the Contract.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Force Majeure in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: Neither party shall be liable for any delay or failure to perform its obligations under this Contract if such delay or failure results from a Force Majeure Event, including but not limited to acts of God, war, terrorism, fire, flood, earthquake, epidemic, pandemic, governmental action, labour disputes, or any other cause beyond the reasonable control of the affected party. The affected party shall promptly notify the other party and use commercially reasonable efforts to resume performance.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Force Majeure in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: In addition to the standard Force Majeure events, the parties acknowledge that pandemic, epidemic, or public health emergency declared by federal, provincial, or municipal authorities, including quarantine orders and mandatory lockdowns, shall constitute Force Majeure. The affected party shall provide notice within five (5) business days and the parties shall negotiate in good faith to adjust schedules and deliverables.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Survival in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The provisions of this Contract relating to confidentiality, intellectual property, indemnification, limitation of liability, audit rights, and any other provision which by its nature is intended to survive, shall survive the expiration or termination of this Contract.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Order of Precedence in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: In the event of any conflict or inconsistency between the documents forming this Contract, the following order of precedence shall apply: (a) the Articles of Agreement; (b) Supplemental General Conditions; (c) General Conditions; (d) the Statement of Work; (e) Annexes and Schedules. The document higher in precedence shall prevail to the extent of the conflict.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Independent Contractor in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor is an independent contractor and nothing in this Contract shall be construed as creating an employment, partnership, joint venture, or agency relationship between the Contractor and Canada. The Contractor shall be solely responsible for all statutory deductions, employment insurance premiums, and Canada Pension Plan contributions for its personnel.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Representations Warranties in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor represents and warrants that: (a) it has the legal capacity and authority to enter into this Contract; (b) it holds all licences and permits required by law; (c) it has the experience, qualifications, and resources to perform the Work; and (d) all information provided in its bid or proposal is accurate and complete in all material respects.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Set Off in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: Canada may set off against any amount payable to the Contractor under this Contract any amount payable by the Contractor to Canada under this Contract or under any other contract or obligation. The Contractor shall be given reasonable notice before any set-off is applied.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Bid Bond in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Bidder shall provide a bid bond or irrevocable standby letter of credit in the amount of ten percent (10%) of the total bid price, issued by a surety licensed to operate in Canada and acceptable to Canada. The bid bond shall remain valid for a minimum of sixty (60) days following bid closing.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Performance Bond in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: Within fourteen (14) calendar days of contract award, the Contractor shall deliver to Canada a performance bond in the amount of fifty percent (50%) of the Contract Price, issued by a surety licensed in Canada and listed on the Treasury Board list of approved sureties. The bond shall remain in force until one (1) year after the date of substantial completion.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Labour Material Bond in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall deliver a labour and material payment bond in the amount of fifty percent (50%) of the Contract Price to protect subcontractors and suppliers. The bond shall be issued by a surety company approved by the Treasury Board of Canada and shall remain valid for the duration of the Contract plus the applicable statutory lien period.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Bid Evaluation in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: Bids will be evaluated on the basis of the highest combined rating of technical merit and price using a weighting of 70% technical and 30% price. Only bids that meet all mandatory technical requirements and achieve a minimum score of 70% on the rated technical criteria will be considered for financial evaluation.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Conflict of Interest in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall not provide any services to any person or entity where such services could give rise to a real, potential, or apparent conflict of interest in relation to the Work under this Contract. The Contractor shall promptly disclose to Canada any situation that may constitute a conflict of interest and Canada shall determine, at its sole discretion, whether a conflict exists and the appropriate remedy.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Subcontracting in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall not subcontract any part of the Work without the prior written consent of the Contracting Authority. The Contractor shall remain fully responsible for the performance of all subcontracted work as if such work were performed by the Contractor directly. Subcontracting does not relieve the Contractor of any obligation under this Contract.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Joint Venture in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: Where a joint venture is proposed, each member of the joint venture shall be jointly and severally liable for the performance of the entire Contract. The joint venture shall designate a lead member authorized to act on behalf of all members. Any change to the composition of the joint venture after contract award requires the prior written approval of Canada.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Canadian Content in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: This procurement is subject to the Canadian Content Policy. The Contractor certifies that the goods and services delivered under this Contract will meet the Canadian Content requirements, meaning not less than eighty percent (80%) of the total bid price shall represent Canadian goods and services as defined in the Supply Manual.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Indigenous Procurement in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: This procurement is set aside under the Procurement Strategy for Aboriginal Business (PSAB). To be eligible, bidders must be Indigenous businesses as defined by Indigenous Services Canada, meaning at least 51% owned and controlled by Indigenous peoples, with at least 33% of the workforce being Indigenous. The Contractor shall maintain compliance with PSAB requirements throughout the Contract term.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Green Procurement in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall comply with the Government of Canada's Policy on Green Procurement and the Federal Sustainable Development Strategy. All deliverables shall minimize environmental impact, including the use of recycled or recyclable materials, energy-efficient equipment, and reduced packaging. The Contractor shall report on carbon footprint and waste reduction measures as part of quarterly reporting.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Security Clearance in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor's personnel who require access to protected or classified information, assets, or sensitive work sites must hold a valid security clearance at the level required (Reliability Status, Secret, or Top Secret) issued by the Canadian Industrial Security Directorate (CISD). The Contractor shall not permit any individual to access such information without the required clearance.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Integrity Provisions in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor certifies that neither the Contractor nor any of its affiliates has been convicted of or charged with offences listed under the Ineligibility and Suspension Policy, including fraud, bribery, collusion, bid-rigging, money laundering, tax evasion, or trafficking. The Contractor shall immediately disclose any change in status and understands that a breach may result in contract termination and a period of ineligibility.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Trade Agreement in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: This procurement is subject to the Canadian Free Trade Agreement (CFTA), Chapter 5 — Government Procurement. In accordance with CFTA Article 504, all Canadian suppliers shall be afforded equal access and non-discriminatory treatment. The procurement thresholds, time limits, and procedures comply with Canada's obligations under the CFTA, including the requirement to publish procurement notices on a single electronic tendering system accessible to all Canadian suppliers.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Trade Agreement in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: Pursuant to CFTA Article 503, the procuring entity shall not discriminate against goods, services, or suppliers of any Canadian province or territory. Technical specifications shall not be prepared, adopted, or applied with the purpose or effect of creating unnecessary obstacles to internal trade. Where reference is made to specific standards, the words 'or equivalent' shall be included.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Trade Agreement in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: Any supplier that believes a procurement covered by the CFTA has not been conducted in accordance with Chapter 5 may file a complaint with the Canadian International Trade Tribunal (CITT) within ten (10) working days of when the basis of the complaint became known or reasonably should have become known, pursuant to CFTA Article 517 and the Canadian International Trade Tribunal Procurement Inquiry Regulations, SOR/93-602.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Trade Agreement in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: Limited tendering (sole-source or non-competitive procurement) may only be used in circumstances permitted under CFTA Article 513, including: (a) no responsive bids received in open tendering; (b) only one supplier capable of performing the work due to exclusive rights; (c) extreme urgency; (d) procurement from an original supplier for additional goods or services; or (e) procurement below the applicable CFTA threshold. The procuring entity shall maintain a record justifying the use of limited tendering.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Trade Agreement in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: This procurement is subject to CFTA Chapter 5 as the estimated value exceeds the applicable threshold: $30,300 CAD for goods, $121,200 CAD for services, and $303,000 CAD for construction (2024-2025 values, adjusted biennially). The procuring entity shall not divide a procurement into separate contracts or use valuation methods to avoid the application of CFTA obligations.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Competition Law in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor certifies that it has not, directly or indirectly, engaged in bid-rigging as defined in section 47 of the Competition Act, RSC 1985, c C-34. Bid-rigging is a criminal offence carrying a maximum penalty of imprisonment for up to fourteen (14) years and/or a fine at the discretion of the court. The Contractor shall not enter into any agreement or arrangement with other bidders regarding the submission or withdrawal of bids, price levels, or allocation of contracts.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Competition Law in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor certifies compliance with section 45 of the Competition Act, RSC 1985, c C-34, and represents that it has not conspired, agreed, or arranged with any competitor to fix prices, allocate markets or customers, or restrict the supply of goods or services in relation to this procurement. A breach of this certification shall constitute grounds for immediate termination of the Contract for default and may result in debarment under the Ineligibility and Suspension Policy.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Competition Law in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor represents that no false or misleading representations have been made in connection with this procurement, in accordance with sections 52 and 74.01 of the Competition Act, RSC 1985, c C-34. The Contractor warrants that all representations made in its bid or proposal, including qualifications of personnel, past performance, and financial capacity, are truthful and not misleading in any material respect.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Competition Law in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor acknowledges that the Competition Bureau of Canada may investigate suspected bid-rigging, price-fixing, or other anti-competitive conduct in government procurement. The Contractor shall cooperate fully with any investigation by the Competition Bureau and shall preserve all records relating to this procurement process. The Contractor shall immediately report to the Contracting Authority any conduct by competitors that the Contractor reasonably believes may constitute a violation of the Competition Act.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Procurement Oversight in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: This procurement is subject to review by the Canadian International Trade Tribunal (CITT) under the Canadian International Trade Tribunal Act, RSC 1985, c 47 (4th Supp). Any potential supplier who believes the procurement has not been conducted in accordance with the applicable trade agreements (CFTA, CETA, WTO GPA, CPTPP) may file a complaint with the CITT. The CITT may make recommendations including re-evaluation, re-tendering, or award of compensation for lost profit or bid preparation costs.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Procurement Oversight in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: Complaints to the Canadian International Trade Tribunal must be filed within ten (10) working days of when the complainant knew or reasonably should have known of the basis for the complaint, in accordance with the CITT Procurement Inquiry Regulations, SOR/93-602. The CITT shall determine within five (5) working days whether to conduct an inquiry. If an inquiry is initiated before contract award, the procuring entity shall delay award for the duration of the inquiry unless urgent operational requirements justify proceeding.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Procurement Oversight in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: Where the CITT determines that a procurement was not conducted in accordance with applicable trade agreement obligations, the Tribunal may recommend that the procuring entity: (a) re-evaluate the bids; (b) terminate the contract and re-tender; (c) award the contract to the complainant; or (d) pay compensation equal to the complainant's reasonable bid preparation costs or, where appropriate, lost profits. The Government of Canada shall implement CITT recommendations within the time specified by the Tribunal.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Procurement Oversight in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: Suppliers may also raise procurement-related concerns with the Office of the Procurement Ombudsman (OPO) established under the Department of Public Works and Government Services Act, RSC 1985, c P-38.2, section 22.1. The OPO reviews procurement practices, investigates complaints from suppliers regarding contract awards below the CITT threshold, and may make non-binding recommendations to improve fairness and transparency in federal procurement.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Commercial Terms in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: Canada shall pay undisputed invoices within thirty (30) calendar days of receipt of a properly rendered invoice. Invoices must include the Contract number, a description of the work performed, the period covered, and the Contractor's GST/HST registration number. Late payments shall accrue interest at the rate prescribed under the Interest and Administrative Charges Regulations, SOR/96-188.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Commercial Terms in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: Progress payments shall be made monthly based on the percentage of Work completed to the satisfaction of the Technical Authority, as certified by the Project Manager. Each progress claim shall be accompanied by supporting documentation including timesheets, expense receipts, and a progress report detailing work completed in the period.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Commercial Terms in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: Canada shall retain a holdback of ten percent (10%) from each progress payment until Substantial Completion of the Work has been certified. The holdback shall be released forty-five (45) days after the date of Substantial Completion, provided that the Contractor has completed all deficiency work and no liens have been registered against the Work under the applicable provincial lien legislation.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Commercial Terms in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: Contract rates shall be subject to annual adjustment on each anniversary of the Contract effective date based on the Consumer Price Index (CPI) for Canada, All Items, as published by Statistics Canada (CANSIM Table 18-10-0004-01). The adjustment shall be calculated as the percentage change in CPI over the preceding twelve (12) months and shall not exceed three percent (3%) in any single year.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Commercial Terms in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contract Price is a firm fixed price of the amount set out in the Articles of Agreement and includes all costs, overhead, profit, and applicable taxes (except GST/HST). The Contractor shall bear all risk of cost overruns. No increase to the Contract Price shall be permitted unless authorized by a formal amendment.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Commercial Terms in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: Payment shall be on a time-and-materials basis at the per diem or hourly rates set out in Schedule B (Basis of Payment). The Contractor shall not exceed the Contract ceiling price without prior written authorization. All expenses must be pre-approved and substantiated with original receipts. Travel expenses shall be reimbursed in accordance with the Treasury Board Travel Directive.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Commercial Terms in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: All prices are exclusive of the Goods and Services Tax (GST) or Harmonized Sales Tax (HST), which shall be shown as a separate line item on all invoices. The Contractor is responsible for all other federal, provincial, and municipal taxes, duties, and levies applicable to the provision of the Work. Where the Contractor is a non-resident, applicable withholding tax under the Income Tax Act, RSC 1985, c 1 (5th Supp) shall apply.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Commercial Terms in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: All prices and payments under this Contract shall be in Canadian dollars (CAD). Where goods or services are sourced internationally, the Contractor shall bear all currency exchange risk. Invoices submitted in any currency other than Canadian dollars will be returned without processing.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Commercial Terms in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: Travel and living expenses shall be reimbursed at cost, without markup, in accordance with the rates and allowances set out in the National Joint Council Travel Directive. All travel must be pre-authorized by the Technical Authority. Air travel shall be at economy class unless otherwise authorized. Claims must be supported by itemized receipts.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Audit Rights in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall maintain complete and accurate records and accounts of all costs and expenditures incurred under this Contract for a period of seven (7) years after final payment. Canada, the Auditor General of Canada, and their authorized representatives shall have the right, on reasonable notice, to inspect, audit, and copy any such records for the purpose of verifying charges and ensuring compliance with the Contract terms.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Commercial Terms in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall submit quarterly financial status reports within fifteen (15) business days of the end of each fiscal quarter. Reports shall include: actual expenditures to date, committed costs, forecast to completion, variance analysis, and a cash flow projection for the remaining Contract period.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Commercial Terms in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall provide a performance guarantee in the form of an irrevocable standby letter of credit equal to fifteen percent (15%) of the total Contract value, drawn on a Canadian Schedule I or Schedule II bank. The guarantee shall remain in force until final acceptance of all deliverables and shall be returned to the Contractor within thirty (30) days thereafter.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Deliverables in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall deliver all services and outputs in accordance with the specifications set out in this Scope of Work, including but not limited to reports, documentation, and system implementations, within the timelines specified in Schedule A.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Timeline Milestones in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: All deliverables must be completed within the timelines specified in Schedule A, and no later than 30 calendar days from the commencement date, unless otherwise approved in writing by Canada.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Acceptance Closure in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: Canada shall have fifteen (15) business days following receipt of each deliverable to conduct acceptance testing in accordance with the acceptance criteria set out in Schedule A. Canada shall provide written notice of acceptance or rejection with specific deficiencies identified. The Contractor shall correct all deficiencies within ten (10) business days at no additional cost.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Performance Standards in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall maintain a minimum service availability of 99.5% measured on a monthly basis, excluding scheduled maintenance windows. The Contractor shall respond to critical incidents (Severity 1) within one (1) hour and resolve within four (4) hours. Service credits equal to 2% of monthly fees per 0.1% below the availability target shall be applied automatically.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Key Personnel in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall assign the key personnel identified in its proposal to perform the Work. The Contractor shall not remove or replace any key personnel without the prior written consent of the Technical Authority. Any proposed replacement must possess qualifications and experience equivalent to or greater than the individual being replaced.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Reporting in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall submit written progress reports to the Technical Authority on a monthly basis, or as otherwise specified in the Statement of Work. Reports shall include: a summary of work completed during the reporting period, work planned for the next period, identification of risks and issues, and status against each milestone in Schedule A.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Quality Assurance in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall implement and maintain a quality assurance program consistent with ISO 9001:2015 or equivalent. The Contractor shall submit its Quality Management Plan for approval within fifteen (15) business days of contract award. Canada reserves the right to conduct quality audits and inspections at any time during the Contract period.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Warranty in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor warrants that all deliverables shall be free from defects in design, materials, and workmanship for a period of twelve (12) months from the date of written acceptance. During the warranty period, the Contractor shall, at no additional cost to Canada, repair or replace any defective deliverable within ten (10) business days of receiving notice.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Warranty in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor warrants that all deliverables, including software and documentation, shall conform to the specifications and be free from defects for a period of twenty-four (24) months from the date of final acceptance. The warranty shall include all patches, updates, and bug fixes necessary to maintain compliance with the specifications at no additional cost to Ontario.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Training in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall provide comprehensive training to Canada's designated personnel on the use, operation, and maintenance of all deliverables. Training shall include hands-on sessions, user manuals, and administrator guides in both official languages. The Contractor shall deliver a minimum of three (3) training sessions of no less than eight (8) hours each.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Transition Services in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: Upon expiry or termination of this Contract, the Contractor shall provide transition services for a period of up to ninety (90) days to facilitate the orderly transfer of responsibilities to Canada or a successor contractor. Transition services shall include complete knowledge transfer, data migration, documentation of processes, and support for parallel operations.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Business Continuity in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall maintain a Business Continuity Plan (BCP) and Disaster Recovery Plan (DRP) that ensure recovery of critical services within four (4) hours (RTO) and data loss of no more than one (1) hour (RPO). The Contractor shall test its BCP/DRP at least annually and provide test results to the Technical Authority within thirty (30) days of each test.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Project Governance in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall participate in a joint project governance structure comprising a Steering Committee (meeting quarterly) and a Project Working Group (meeting bi-weekly). The Contractor's Project Manager shall serve as the primary point of contact and shall have authority to make decisions on behalf of the Contractor regarding day-to-day project matters.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Change Management in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: Any change to the scope of work set out in this Contract must be requested in writing and approved by both parties prior to implementation. The Contractor shall provide a written impact assessment, including any changes to cost, timeline, or deliverables, within five (5) business days of receiving a change request.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Liquidated Damages in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: If the Contractor fails to deliver any deliverable by the date specified in Schedule A, Canada shall be entitled to liquidated damages equal to one percent (1%) of the value of the late deliverable for each week of delay, up to a maximum of ten percent (10%) of the total Contract Price. The parties agree that this amount represents a genuine pre-estimate of the damages Canada would suffer.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Risk Allocation in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall indemnify and hold harmless Canada, its officers, employees, and agents from and against any and all claims, damages, losses, costs, and expenses arising out of or related to: (a) any breach by the Contractor of this Contract; (b) any negligent or wrongful act or omission of the Contractor; or (c) any infringement of intellectual property rights by the Contractor.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Risk Allocation in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall defend, indemnify, and hold harmless Canada from any third-party claim alleging that the Deliverables or Canada's use thereof infringes any patent, copyright, trademark, or trade secret. The Contractor's obligation is conditional on Canada providing prompt written notice, granting the Contractor sole control of the defence, and providing reasonable assistance at the Contractor's expense.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Risk Allocation in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: Neither party shall be liable to the other for any indirect, incidental, special, exemplary, or consequential damages, including loss of revenue, loss of profits, loss of data, or loss of goodwill. Each party's aggregate liability arising out of this Contract shall not exceed the total fees paid or payable in the twelve (12) months preceding the event giving rise to the claim.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Risk Allocation in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The limitations on liability shall not apply to: (a) fraud or wilful misconduct; (b) gross negligence; (c) indemnification obligations under this Contract; (d) breaches of confidentiality; (e) breaches of intellectual property provisions; or (f) any liability that cannot be limited by applicable law including the Crown Liability and Proceedings Act, RSC 1985, c C-50.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Insurance in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall obtain and maintain professional liability (errors and omissions) insurance with a minimum limit of two million dollars ($2,000,000) per occurrence and in the aggregate. The policy shall be maintained for the duration of the Contract and for a period of not less than two (2) years following completion of the Work. Canada shall be named as an additional insured.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Insurance in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall obtain and maintain commercial general liability insurance with a minimum limit of five million dollars ($5,000,000) per occurrence, covering bodily injury, property damage, personal injury, and products and completed operations. The policy shall include a cross-liability clause and Canada shall be named as an additional insured.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Insurance in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall obtain and maintain cyber liability insurance with a minimum limit of one million dollars ($1,000,000) covering data breach response costs, network security liability, media liability, regulatory fines and penalties, and cyber extortion. The policy shall cover both first-party and third-party losses arising from a cyber security incident.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Insurance in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall comply with all applicable provincial and territorial workers' compensation legislation and shall maintain coverage for all its employees and subcontractors engaged in the Work. The Contractor shall provide a valid clearance certificate from the applicable Workers' Safety and Insurance Board (WSIB) or equivalent authority prior to commencing work.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Insurance in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: For professional services engagements, the Contractor shall maintain errors and omissions insurance with a minimum limit of two million dollars ($2,000,000) per claim, covering negligent acts, errors, or omissions in the performance of professional services under this Contract. The policy shall have a retroactive date no later than the Contract commencement date.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Insurance in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: Where the Work involves the supply of goods or manufactured products, the Contractor shall maintain product liability insurance with a minimum limit of five million dollars ($5,000,000) per occurrence covering damages arising from defects in products supplied under this Contract. Coverage shall extend to the completed operations period for a minimum of two (2) years following final delivery.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Intellectual Property in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: All intellectual property created, developed, or first reduced to practice by the Contractor in the performance of this Contract shall vest in and be owned exclusively by Canada upon creation. The Contractor hereby assigns to Canada all right, title, and interest in such intellectual property and agrees to execute any further instruments required to perfect such assignment.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Intellectual Property in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor hereby grants to Canada a non-exclusive, irrevocable, royalty-free, worldwide licence to use, reproduce, modify, and distribute any pre-existing intellectual property of the Contractor that is incorporated into or necessary for Canada to use the Deliverables for the purposes contemplated by this Contract.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Intellectual Property in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall disclose all open-source software components incorporated into the Deliverables, including the applicable licence terms (GPL, MIT, Apache, etc.). The Contractor warrants that its use of open-source software does not create any obligation on Canada to release proprietary source code or pay royalties. A software bill of materials (SBOM) shall be provided with each release.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Data Ownership in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: All data created, collected, or processed by the Contractor in the performance of this Contract is and shall remain the exclusive property of Canada. The Contractor shall not use such data for any purpose other than performing its obligations under this Contract and shall return or securely destroy all data within thirty (30) days of contract expiry or termination.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Data Portability in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall ensure that all data stored or processed under this Contract can be exported in a standard, machine-readable, non-proprietary format (CSV, JSON, XML, or equivalent) at any time during or after the Contract period. The Contractor shall not impose any technical barriers, fees, or delays that impede data portability.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Source Code Escrow in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: Where the Deliverables include proprietary software, the Contractor shall deposit the source code, build scripts, and documentation with a mutually acceptable escrow agent. Canada shall be entitled to access the escrowed materials upon: (a) the Contractor's insolvency or bankruptcy; (b) the Contractor's failure to maintain or support the software; or (c) termination of the Contract for default.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Software Licensing in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: Where the Contractor provides software under licence, the licence shall be non-exclusive, perpetual, irrevocable, and royalty-free for Canada's use for government purposes. The licence shall permit unlimited users within the federal government and shall not be subject to audit or compliance review by the Contractor without thirty (30) days advance notice.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Data Residency in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: All data, including personal information and Protected B information, shall be stored, processed, and backed up exclusively within the geographic boundaries of Canada. The Contractor shall not transfer, store, or process any data outside Canada without the prior written consent of Canada. Cloud infrastructure must be hosted in Canadian data centres operated by providers with a Canadian presence.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Data Breach in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall notify Canada within twenty-four (24) hours of discovering any breach, unauthorized access, or loss of data processed under this Contract. The notification shall include the nature and scope of the breach, the data affected, actions taken to contain the breach, and a remediation plan. The Contractor shall bear all costs of breach response including notification to affected individuals, credit monitoring, and forensic investigation.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Data Retention in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall retain all data and records in accordance with the retention periods set out in Schedule C or as required by applicable law. Upon expiry of the retention period, or within thirty (30) days of written request by Canada, the Contractor shall securely destroy all data using methods consistent with ITSG-06 (Clearing and Declassifying Electronic Data Storage Devices) and provide a certificate of destruction.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for API Access in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall provide fully documented RESTful API access to all data and functionality delivered under this Contract. APIs shall conform to the Government of Canada API Standards and shall be available at no additional cost for integration with other government systems. The Contractor shall maintain backward compatibility for a minimum of twelve (12) months following any API version change.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Technology Transfer in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall transfer sufficient technical knowledge to enable Canada's staff to independently operate, maintain, and enhance the Deliverables following the Contract period. Technology transfer shall include source code, configuration files, architecture documentation, runbooks, and a minimum of forty (40) hours of hands-on training.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Compliance Regulatory in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall comply with the Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act, SC 2000, c 5, and the Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act, RSO 1990, c F-31, in its collection, use, and disclosure of personal information under this Contract.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Compliance Regulatory in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor acknowledges that records created or received in the performance of this Contract may be subject to the Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act, RSO 1990, c F-31 (FIPPA). The Contractor shall assist Ontario in responding to access requests and shall not destroy any records that may be subject to FIPPA without the prior written approval of Ontario.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Compliance Regulatory in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: All deliverables, systems, and digital content provided under this Contract must comply with the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act, 2005, SO 2005, c 11 (AODA), including the Integrated Accessibility Standards Regulation, O Reg 191/11, and meet or exceed WCAG 2.1 Level AA conformance for all web-based content and applications.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Compliance Regulatory in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: All deliverables, reports, user interfaces, and public-facing materials shall be provided in both official languages (English and French) in accordance with the Official Languages Act, RSC 1985, c 31 (4th Supp). Translation shall be of professional quality and subject to review by Canada's official languages coordinator.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Compliance Regulatory in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor represents and warrants that neither the Contractor nor any of its agents, employees, or subcontractors has, directly or indirectly, paid, offered, or promised any bribe, gift, or other improper advantage to any public official in connection with this Contract, in violation of the Corruption of Foreign Public Officials Act, SC 1998, c 34 (CFPOA) or the Criminal Code, RSC 1985, c C-46.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Compliance Regulatory in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall comply with all applicable Canadian export control laws including the Export and Import Permits Act, RSC 1985, c E-19, the Defence Production Act, RSC 1985, c D-1, and the Special Economic Measures Act, SC 1992, c 17. The Contractor shall not export or re-export any controlled goods, technology, or technical data without the required permits.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Compliance Regulatory in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall comply with all applicable federal and provincial employment and labour standards legislation, including the Canada Labour Code, RSC 1985, c L-2, the Employment Standards Act, 2000, SO 2000, c 41, and all applicable collective bargaining agreements. The Contractor shall pay all workers at or above minimum wage and shall not engage in any form of forced or child labour.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Compliance Regulatory in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall comply with the Occupational Health and Safety Act, RSO 1990, c O.1, and all applicable workplace health and safety regulations. The Contractor shall maintain a health and safety program, provide all necessary personal protective equipment, and report all workplace incidents to the appropriate authorities and to the Technical Authority within twenty-four (24) hours.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Compliance Regulatory in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall comply with all applicable federal and provincial environmental legislation, including the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999, SC 1999, c 33, the Impact Assessment Act, SC 2019, c 28, and the Transportation of Dangerous Goods Act, 1992, SC 1992, c 34. The Contractor shall be responsible for the proper handling, storage, and disposal of all hazardous materials.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Compliance Regulatory in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall comply with the Pay Equity Act, SC 2018, c 27, s 416, and shall ensure equal pay for work of equal value within its organization. For contracts valued at $1,000,000 or more, the Contractor shall provide certification of compliance with the Federal Contractors Program for Employment Equity, as required by the Employment Equity Act, SC 1995, c 44.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Compliance Regulatory in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor represents that it has implemented measures to identify and mitigate the risk of forced labour and child labour in its supply chains, in accordance with the Fighting Against Forced Labour and Child Labour in Supply Chains Act, SC 2023, c 9. The Contractor shall, upon request, provide Canada with its most recent report filed under the Act.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Compliance Regulatory in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor certifies that neither the Contractor nor any of its directors, officers, or beneficial owners is a designated person under the Special Economic Measures Act, SC 1992, c 17, the Justice for Victims of Corrupt Foreign Officials Act, SC 2017, c 21, or the United Nations Act, RSC 1985, c U-2. The Contractor shall not engage any sanctioned entity in the performance of this Contract.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Termination in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: Canada may, at any time, terminate this Contract for convenience by providing thirty (30) calendar days' written notice to the Contractor. Upon termination for convenience, Canada's liability is limited to payment for work satisfactorily performed and accepted prior to the termination date, together with reasonable and substantiated wind-down costs.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Termination in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: Canada may terminate this Contract for default if the Contractor: (a) fails to perform any material obligation and does not cure within fifteen (15) business days after written notice; (b) becomes insolvent or makes an assignment for the benefit of creditors; or (c) is convicted of a criminal offence under the Integrity Provisions. Upon termination for default, Canada reserves all rights and remedies available at law and in equity.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Termination in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: Canada may immediately terminate this Contract, without notice or further obligation, if the Contractor: (a) becomes bankrupt or insolvent; (b) makes a proposal under the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act, RSC 1985, c B-3; (c) has a receiver or receiver-manager appointed over its assets; or (d) is subject to proceedings under the Companies' Creditors Arrangement Act, RSC 1985, c C-36.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Termination in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: This Contract may be terminated at any time by the mutual written agreement of both parties. The parties shall negotiate in good faith the terms of the termination, including payment for work completed, return of materials and data, and any transition obligations. A mutual termination agreement shall be set out in a formal amendment.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Suspension in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: Canada may, at any time, by written notice, order the Contractor to suspend all or any part of the Work for a period of up to one hundred and eighty (180) days. During the suspension, the Contractor shall protect and preserve the Work already performed. If the suspension exceeds one hundred and eighty (180) days, the Contractor may request termination for convenience and Canada shall compensate the Contractor for demonstrated costs of the suspension.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Dispute Resolution in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: In the event of a dispute arising out of this Contract, the parties shall first attempt resolution through good-faith negotiation between the respective Project Managers for a period of ten (10) business days. If unresolved, the dispute shall be escalated to the respective senior executives for a further period of twenty (20) business days before either party may pursue formal dispute resolution.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Dispute Resolution in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: If a dispute is not resolved through escalation, either party may refer the matter to mediation administered by a mutually agreed mediator or, failing agreement, a mediator appointed by the ADR Institute of Ontario. The costs of mediation shall be shared equally. Mediation shall be conducted in accordance with the National Mediation Rules of the ADR Institute of Canada.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Dispute Resolution in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: If mediation fails, either party may refer the dispute to binding arbitration under the Arbitration Act, 1991, SO 1991, c 17. The arbitration shall be conducted by a single arbitrator in Toronto, Ontario, in the English language. The arbitrator's award shall be final and binding on the parties and may be entered as a judgment in any court of competent jurisdiction.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Dispute Resolution in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: Nothing in this Contract shall be construed as limiting the right of either party to seek injunctive or equitable relief from a court of competent jurisdiction where irreparable harm would result from the breach. For federal Crown contracts, disputes may be referred to the Canadian International Trade Tribunal (CITT) for procurement-related complaints under the Canadian International Trade Tribunal Act, RSC 1985, c 47 (4th Supp).

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Transition in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: Upon expiry or termination of this Contract for any reason, the Contractor shall: (a) cooperate with Canada and any successor contractor for a transition period of up to ninety (90) days; (b) transfer all data, documentation, and work product to Canada; (c) provide knowledge transfer sessions; (d) continue to provide critical services as directed during the transition; and (e) return or destroy all confidential information of Canada.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Cloud SaaS in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall deliver the Software as a Service (SaaS) solution in accordance with the service levels set out in Schedule B. The Contractor shall provide a minimum of twelve (12) months' notice prior to end-of-life or discontinuation of the service. Upon termination, the Contractor shall provide data export in a standard format and maintain read-only access for a minimum of ninety (90) days.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Agile Development in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall deliver the Work using an Agile methodology with two-week sprint cycles. Each sprint shall produce potentially shippable increments of functionality as defined in the prioritized product backlog. Sprint planning, daily stand-ups, sprint reviews, and retrospectives shall include participation by Canada's Product Owner. Acceptance of each sprint deliverable shall be confirmed by the Product Owner within five (5) business days.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Software Maintenance in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall provide software maintenance and support services for the duration of the Contract, including: (a) critical security patches within twenty-four (24) hours; (b) bug fixes within five (5) business days for Severity 1 and ten (10) business days for Severity 2; (c) quarterly minor releases incorporating approved enhancements; and (d) annual major releases with backward compatibility for a minimum of twelve (12) months.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Data Migration in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall develop and execute a data migration plan to transfer all existing data from the legacy system to the new solution. The plan shall include data mapping, validation rules, cleansing procedures, and rollback strategy. The Contractor shall perform a minimum of two (2) trial migrations before the production migration and shall achieve a data migration accuracy rate of 99.9% or higher.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Substantial Completion in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: Substantial Completion shall be achieved when the Work is ready for its intended use and complies with all codes and regulations, notwithstanding the existence of minor deficiencies that do not materially affect the use of the Work. The Contractor shall provide notice of Substantial Completion and Canada shall inspect within ten (10) business days. A deficiency list shall be prepared and the Contractor shall complete all deficiency work within thirty (30) days.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Deficiency List in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: Upon notification of Substantial Completion, the Technical Authority shall prepare a comprehensive deficiency list identifying all items of Work that are incomplete, defective, or not in conformance with the Contract documents. The Contractor shall rectify all deficiency items within thirty (30) calendar days at no additional cost. Failure to complete deficiency work within this period may result in Canada engaging others to complete the work and deducting the cost from amounts owing.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Site Safety in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor is the constructor as defined in the Occupational Health and Safety Act, RSO 1990, c O.1, and shall be responsible for all health and safety matters at the work site. The Contractor shall appoint a competent supervisor, maintain a site-specific safety plan, conduct daily toolbox talks, and ensure all workers have completed mandatory health and safety training including Working at Heights where applicable.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Builders Lien in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall comply with the Construction Act, RSO 1990, c C.30, including all obligations respecting holdbacks, liens, and prompt payment. The Contractor shall ensure that all subcontractors and suppliers are paid in accordance with the prompt payment provisions and shall provide statutory declarations confirming payment prior to the release of any holdback.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Professional Standards in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall perform the Work in accordance with the professional standards, practices, and codes of conduct applicable to the Contractor's profession and industry. The Contractor shall ensure that all personnel assigned to the Work hold the required professional designations, certifications, and memberships in good standing with the applicable regulatory body.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Conflict Disclosure in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall disclose to Canada, prior to commencing work and on an ongoing basis, any assignment, contract, or business relationship that could give rise to a real, potential, or apparent conflict of interest. The Contractor shall not provide advice to Canada on any matter in which the Contractor has a direct or indirect financial interest without full disclosure and written consent.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Deliverable Ownership in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: All reports, analyses, recommendations, presentations, data compilations, and other work product prepared by the Contractor in the course of this engagement shall be the exclusive property of Canada. The Contractor shall not publish, present, or otherwise disclose the findings or recommendations without the prior written consent of Canada.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Staff Augmentation in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall provide qualified personnel to supplement Canada's project team as described in the Statement of Work. Personnel shall work under the day-to-day direction of Canada's Project Manager while remaining employees of the Contractor. The Contractor shall replace any resource within ten (10) business days if Canada determines, acting reasonably, that the resource does not meet the required competency level.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for International Procurement in International jurisdiction.
output: This procurement is conducted in accordance with the principles of the UNCITRAL Model Law on Public Procurement (2011), ensuring transparency, competition, fairness, and economy in the procurement process. All bidders shall receive equal treatment and the evaluation criteria shall be disclosed in advance. The procuring entity shall maintain a record of the procurement proceedings.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for International Procurement in International jurisdiction.
output: This procurement is subject to the World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF Borrowers (4th Edition, November 2020). The Contractor shall comply with the World Bank's Anti-Corruption Guidelines and Sanctions Framework. The World Bank reserves the right to audit all procurement activities and the Contractor shall maintain records for a minimum of seven (7) years following contract completion.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for International Procurement in EU jurisdiction.
output: This procurement is conducted in accordance with Directive 2014/24/EU of the European Parliament on public procurement. The Contract Notice has been published in the Official Journal of the European Union (OJEU) via TED (Tenders Electronic Daily). All economic operators from EU and EEA member states are eligible to participate. Evaluation shall follow the most economically advantageous tender (MEAT) criteria.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for International Procurement in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: This procurement is subject to the Canada-European Union Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA), Chapter 19 — Government Procurement. Suppliers from EU member states shall be afforded national treatment and non-discrimination in accordance with CETA obligations. The procurement thresholds and procedures comply with Canada's commitments under CETA Annex 19-1.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for International Procurement in CA-Federal jurisdiction.
output: This procurement is subject to the World Trade Organization Agreement on Government Procurement (GPA), as revised in 2012. Suppliers from GPA member states shall be afforded treatment no less favourable than domestic suppliers. The procurement notice has been published in accordance with WTO GPA transparency requirements and the applicable time limits for tendering have been observed.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for International Procurement in International jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor certifies compliance with the OECD Convention on Combating Bribery of Foreign Public Officials in International Business Transactions and the corresponding Canadian implementing legislation, the Corruption of Foreign Public Officials Act, SC 1998, c 34. The Contractor shall maintain an anti-bribery compliance program including training, risk assessments, and reporting mechanisms.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for International Procurement in International jurisdiction.
output: Where personal data is transferred between Canada and the European Union, such transfers shall comply with the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), Regulation (EU) 2016/679, and may only be made on the basis of an adequacy decision, standard contractual clauses approved by the European Commission, or binding corporate rules. The Contractor shall implement appropriate safeguards as required by Article 46 of the GDPR.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Dispute Resolution in International jurisdiction.
output: Any dispute arising out of or in connection with this Contract shall be finally settled under the Rules of Arbitration of the International Chamber of Commerce (ICC) by one or three arbitrators appointed in accordance with the said Rules. The seat of arbitration shall be Toronto, Canada. The language of the arbitration shall be English. The arbitral award shall be final and binding and may be enforced in any jurisdiction under the New York Convention.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for International Procurement in International jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall align its performance under this Contract with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), in particular: Goal 8 (Decent Work and Economic Growth), Goal 12 (Responsible Consumption and Production), and Goal 16 (Peace, Justice, and Strong Institutions). The Contractor shall report annually on measures taken to advance these goals.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for International Procurement in International jurisdiction.
output: The Contractor shall conduct human rights due diligence in accordance with the UN Guiding Principles on Business and Human Rights (2011). The Contractor shall identify, prevent, mitigate, and account for adverse human rights impacts in its operations and supply chain. The Contractor shall establish a grievance mechanism accessible to affected stakeholders and shall remediate any adverse impacts identified.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Representations Warranties in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: The Vendor represents and warrants to the Purchaser that: (a) the Vendor is the registered and beneficial owner of the Shares free and clear of all liens, encumbrances, and third-party interests; (b) the Corporation is duly incorporated and in good standing under the laws of Ontario; (c) the financial statements fairly present the financial position of the Corporation as at the date thereof in accordance with GAAP; and (d) there are no pending or threatened actions, suits, or proceedings against the Corporation.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Representations Warranties in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: The Purchaser represents and warrants to the Vendor that: (a) the Purchaser has the corporate power and authority to enter into this Agreement and to complete the transactions contemplated hereby; (b) this Agreement has been duly authorized, executed, and delivered by the Purchaser and constitutes a legal, valid, and binding obligation; and (c) the Purchaser has sufficient funds available to pay the Purchase Price at Closing.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Indemnification in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: The Vendor shall indemnify and hold harmless the Purchaser from and against all losses, damages, claims, costs, and expenses arising from any breach of the Vendor's representations, warranties, or covenants in this Agreement. The Vendor's aggregate liability shall not exceed the Purchase Price. Claims must be notified within eighteen (18) months of the Closing Date, except for claims relating to tax or title to the Shares, which survive indefinitely.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Closing Conditions in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: The obligation of the Purchaser to complete the purchase of the Shares is subject to the satisfaction or waiver of the following conditions: (a) all representations and warranties of the Vendor are true and correct at Closing; (b) the Vendor has performed all covenants required prior to Closing; (c) all required regulatory approvals have been obtained including under the Competition Act, RSC 1985, c C-34; and (d) no material adverse change has occurred since the date of this Agreement.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Material Adverse Change in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: Material Adverse Change means any change, event, or occurrence that, individually or in the aggregate, has or would reasonably be expected to have a material adverse effect on the business, assets, liabilities, financial condition, or results of operations of the Corporation, but excludes changes arising from: (a) general economic or market conditions; (b) changes in applicable law; (c) changes affecting the industry generally; or (d) the announcement of this Agreement.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Shareholder Rights in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: If shareholders holding not less than sixty-six and two-thirds percent (66.67%) of the issued and outstanding shares (the 'Majority Shareholders') accept a bona fide offer from a third party to purchase all of the shares, the Majority Shareholders shall have the right to require the remaining shareholders to sell their shares on the same terms and conditions. The remaining shareholders shall execute all documents necessary to complete such sale within fifteen (15) business days.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Shareholder Rights in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: If any shareholder (the 'Selling Shareholder') proposes to sell or transfer shares to a third party, each other shareholder shall have the right (but not the obligation) to participate in such sale on a pro rata basis and on the same terms and conditions offered to the Selling Shareholder. The Selling Shareholder shall provide not less than thirty (30) days' written notice of the proposed sale.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Shareholder Rights in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: Any shareholder (the 'Offeror') may at any time deliver a notice to the other shareholders (the 'Offerees') offering to purchase all of the Offerees' shares at a price per share specified in the notice. Within thirty (30) days of receipt, each Offeree must either: (a) accept the offer and sell; or (b) purchase all of the Offeror's shares at the same price per share. Failure to respond within the thirty-day period shall be deemed acceptance of the offer.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Shareholder Rights in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: Before any shareholder may sell, transfer, or otherwise dispose of any shares, the shareholder shall first offer such shares to the other shareholders on the same terms and conditions as the proposed transfer. The other shareholders shall have thirty (30) days to accept the offer in whole or in part, pro rata to their existing shareholdings. If the offer is not accepted, the selling shareholder may complete the transfer to the third party within sixty (60) days on terms no more favourable.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Shareholder Rights in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: Pursuant to section 146 of the Business Corporations Act, RSO 1990, c B.16, the shareholders unanimously agree to restrict the powers of the directors to manage or supervise the management of the business and affairs of the Corporation. The following matters shall require the unanimous written consent of all shareholders: (a) any amendment to the articles; (b) the issuance of additional shares; (c) any sale of substantially all assets; (d) any borrowing in excess of $100,000; and (e) the declaration of dividends.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Guarantee in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: The Guarantor unconditionally and irrevocably guarantees to the Creditor the due and punctual payment and performance of all present and future obligations of the Debtor under the Agreement. This guarantee is a continuing guarantee and shall remain in full force until all obligations are satisfied. The Guarantor waives all rights of subrogation until the Creditor has been paid in full. The Creditor may proceed directly against the Guarantor without first pursuing remedies against the Debtor.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Promissory Note in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: FOR VALUE RECEIVED, the undersigned (the 'Maker') promises to pay to the order of the Payee the principal sum of [AMOUNT] Canadian dollars, together with interest thereon at the rate of [X]% per annum, calculated and compounded monthly, payable on demand. Upon default in payment, interest shall accrue at the rate of [X+2]% per annum. The Maker waives presentment, demand, protest, and notice of dishonour. This Note shall be governed by the laws of Ontario and the Bills of Exchange Act, RSC 1985, c B-4.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Non Competition in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: For a period of two (2) years following the Closing Date, the Vendor shall not, directly or indirectly, within a radius of 100 kilometres of any location where the Business is carried on, engage in, carry on, or be employed by any business that competes with the Business. The parties acknowledge that this restriction is reasonable and necessary to protect the goodwill purchased by the Purchaser and the consideration paid therefor.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Non Solicitation in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: For a period of two (2) years following the Closing Date, the Vendor shall not, directly or indirectly: (a) solicit, canvass, or accept business from any customer or client of the Business; or (b) recruit, hire, solicit, or induce any employee, contractor, or consultant of the Business to leave their engagement. This restriction applies to customers and employees with whom the Vendor had material contact during the twelve (12) months prior to Closing.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Employment in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: The Employer may terminate this Agreement without cause by providing written notice or pay in lieu of notice in accordance with the Employment Standards Act, 2000, SO 2000, c 41, and the common law. The Employee shall be entitled to the greater of: (a) the minimum notice or termination pay required under the ESA; or (b) reasonable notice at common law, having regard to the Employee's age, length of service, character of employment, and availability of similar employment.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Employment in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: The Employer may terminate this Agreement immediately for just cause without notice or pay in lieu thereof. Just cause includes but is not limited to: (a) dishonesty, fraud, or theft; (b) wilful misconduct or insubordination; (c) material breach of this Agreement or Company policies; (d) conviction of a criminal offence relevant to the Employee's duties; or (e) conduct that brings the Employer into disrepute. The Employer shall provide the Employee with written particulars of the cause relied upon.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Employment in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: Notwithstanding any other provision of this Agreement, in no event shall the Employee receive less than the minimum entitlements to notice of termination, termination pay, and severance pay (if applicable) required under the Employment Standards Act, 2000, SO 2000, c 41, as amended. Any provision of this Agreement that provides less than the ESA minimum shall be deemed to be amended to provide the ESA minimum.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Employment in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: During the term of employment and for a period of twelve (12) months following the date of termination for any reason, the Employee shall not, directly or indirectly, within the Province of Ontario, engage in or carry on any business that is competitive with the Business of the Employer. The Employee acknowledges that this restriction is reasonable in scope, duration, and geographic extent and is necessary to protect the Employer's legitimate business interests including trade secrets and customer relationships.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Employment in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: For a period of twelve (12) months following termination, the Employee shall not: (a) solicit or attempt to solicit any customer, client, or prospective client of the Employer with whom the Employee had material dealings; or (b) recruit, solicit, or induce any employee or contractor of the Employer to leave the Employer's employment or engagement. The Employee acknowledges receiving adequate consideration for this covenant.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Intellectual Property in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: All inventions, works, designs, software, trade secrets, and other intellectual property created, conceived, or developed by the Employee during the course of employment, or using the Employer's resources, information, or facilities, shall be the exclusive property of the Employer. The Employee hereby assigns to the Employer all right, title, and interest in such intellectual property and agrees to execute all documents necessary to perfect such assignment. The Employee waives all moral rights pursuant to the Copyright Act, RSC 1985, c C-42.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Employment in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: The Employee's employment shall be subject to a probationary period of three (3) months commencing on the start date. During the probationary period, the Employer may terminate this Agreement by providing one (1) week's written notice or pay in lieu thereof. Upon successful completion of the probationary period, the Employee shall be confirmed in the position and shall be entitled to the full benefits of employment.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Employment in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: In consideration of the severance payment described herein, the Employee hereby releases and forever discharges the Employer, its directors, officers, employees, and agents from any and all claims, actions, causes of action, demands, and complaints arising out of or in connection with the employment or its termination, including claims under the Employment Standards Act, the Human Rights Code, RSO 1990, c H.19, and at common law. The Employee acknowledges having had the opportunity to obtain independent legal advice.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Employment in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: The Employee may perform work remotely from a location approved by the Employer. The Employee shall maintain a dedicated workspace that meets health and safety requirements, ensure the security of Company data and equipment, and be available during core business hours. The Employer shall provide necessary equipment and reimburse reasonable expenses. Either party may revoke the remote work arrangement with thirty (30) days' notice.

instruction: Provide standard procurement clause language for Employment in CA-ON jurisdiction.
output: The Employee agrees to comply with all policies, procedures, and codes of conduct of the Employer as amended from time to time, including the Workplace Harassment Policy, Privacy Policy, Acceptable Use Policy, and Code of Ethics.
